task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function correctly evaluates the balance of parentheses in a string, meeting all the specified criteria.
trace: To solve this problem, I used a stack data structure to keep track of opening parentheses in the input string. For each character encountered, if it's an opening parenthesis, we append it to the stack. If it's a closing parenthesis, we check whether the stack is empty or its last element is not an opening parenthesis. In this case, we return False indicating that the parentheses are not balanced. Lastly, if the stack is empty after processing the entire string, all parentheses are balanced and we return True. This approach ensures correctness while keeping the code concise and readable. I also handled an edge case where an empty string is considered balanced.
